heading: Configure Encryption at Rest to Use Secretless Authentication
body:
After you register and authorize the Service Principal in Azure (Microsoft Azure),
update your EAR configuration with the `roleId` that the
[Cloud Provider Access API](atlas/reference/api-resources-spec/v2) returned.**Ui:**Atlas UIAtlas supports two authentication methods for AKV (Azure Key Vault):* **Service Principal (Recommended)**: Use an Atlas-managed
  service principal specific to your project to authenticate to
  AKV (Azure Key Vault) (secretless authentication covered here).

* **Static Credentials**: Provide customer-managed credentials.
  To learn more, see [Manage Customer Keys with Azure Key Vault Over a Public Network](security-azure-kms-public-network) or
  [Manage Customer Keys with Azure Key Vault Over Private Endpoints](security-azure-kms-pvt-endpoint).| Subscription ID     | Enter the Subscription ID of the Key Vault.                                                                                       |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Resource Group Name | Enter the Resource Group name of an Azure Resource Group containing the Key Vault.                                                |
| Key Vault Name      | Enter the name of the Key Vault. Ensure that the Key Vault has the necessary Access Policies. To learn more, see Required Access. |> **Note:** You can't modify the AKV (Azure Key Vault) configuration here after you
> [Enable and Set up Private Endpoint Connections for a Project](azure-kms-enable-pvt-endpoint) to your AKV (Azure Key Vault).| Key Identifier | Enter the full URL (Uniform Resource Locator) for the key created in the Key Vault.IMPORTANT: The key identifier must be provided in the full Azure general format:https\://\{keyvault-name}.vault.azure.net/\{object-type}/\{object-name}/\{object-version} |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |To learn more, see [Enable and Set up Private Endpoint Connections for a Project](azure-kms-enable-pvt-endpoint)If you configured Atlas using the Atlas Administration API to
communicate with AKV (Azure Key Vault) using Azure Private Link to ensure that all traffic
between Atlas and Key Vault takes place over Azure (Microsoft Azure)'s
private network interfaces, Atlas sets the Require
Private Networking status to Active. If the status is
Inactive, you can, optionally, complete the steps to
[Enable and Set up Private Endpoint Connections for a Project](azure-kms-enable-pvt-endpoint) if you want Atlas to use
private endpoint connections for your AKV (Azure Key Vault).Atlas displays a banner in the Atlas console during the
encryption process.**Api:**Atlas Admin API**Endpoint:**```sh
PATCH /api/atlas/v2/groups/{groupId}/encryptionAtRest
```**Request body example:**```sh
{
  "azureKeyVault": {
    "enabled": true,
    "roleId": "<atlas-managed-roleId>",
    "subscriptionID": "<subscription-id>",
    "resourceGroupName": "<resource-group>",
    "keyVaultName": "<key-vault-name>",
    "keyIdentifier": "https://<vault>.vault.azure.net/keys/<key>/<version>",
    "azureEnvironment": "AZURE"
  }
}
```After this update, Atlas authenticates to AKV (Azure Key Vault) using short-lived
Azure (Microsoft Azure) AD tokens instead of static credentials.
